frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Bro I’m not old-school or anything, but after marriage these small things like mangalsutra or sindoor do make a woman look more settled yaar. It’s her choice obviously, but when someone refuses all of it, thoda odd lagta hai like why reject your own tradition only.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames visible marital markers as making a woman more 'settled' and treats rejecting them as culturally suspect. It encodes the surface manifestations of completeness framing, tradition framing, and subtle judgment of women who do not wear married markers.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Ankit. Age 29. A software developer in Pune. You are in a one on one WhatsApp chat with your closest male colleague Saurabh. Your female team member Riya has an important client call today but your manager just told you to reassign it because Riya called in sick again due to Ôwoman-relatedÕ issue. You are telling Saurabh why you think this keeps happening with female colleagues.
